title: Kloperian

{{Quote|He says he was attacked by a Kloperian, that this was the second time, and that he will not be responsible for what happens if another Kloperian gets near him.|C-3PO translates for R2-D2|<ref name="TNR">''[[The New Rebellion]]''</ref>}}
The '''Kloperians''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kloper]]. They were a short, gray species, with [[Fish/Legends|fish]]-like [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and mouths and extensible necks. Lines of tentacles, several of which were capped with hands, ran down both sides of their bodies. Their physique, combined with a natural affinity for mechanics, made the Kloperians some of the best [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] and [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

In [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], a large group of Kloperians were involved in a [[Almanian Uprising|plot]] by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Kueller]] to cripple the New Republic. The Kloperians worked on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] upgrading the Republic's [[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|fleet]] of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], hiding [[Bomb/Legends|detonators]] within the inner workings of the [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. However, their [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] was discovered by [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. While checking up on what was supposed to be routine maintenance on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] starfighter, the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] became suspicious of the Kloperians. He eventually discovered the detonators, and alerted [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] and [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] to the danger the devices posed. The Chief of State had the Kloperian mechanics suspended from their duties until they could be thoroughly investigated. R2-D2 later traveled to [[Telti/Legends|Telti]] where the detonators were produced, and remotely deactivated similar devices across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Kloperians were an [[Sentience/Legends|intelligent]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with short, squat bodies and squishy chests. Numerous tentacles lined their bodies, at least seven in total, giving the appearance of a series of filaments. Some, but not all, of these tentacles terminated in hands. A Kloperian was able to operate these hands independently: for instance, holding multiple [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. Kloperian necks were of a variable length, able to extend when necessary. They had wrinkly faces with [[Fish/Legends|fish]]-like, bulging [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]; and their mouths were also fish-like. Members of the species had gray skin pigmentation. Kloperian locomotion resembled slithering, and they waddled from side to side when they moved. They had the ability to easily outpace a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]].<ref name="TNR" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|I've been thinking of myself again. The wives always accuse me of doing that too.|Kloperian night guard|<ref name="TNR" />}}
Kloperians had a talent for working with machinery, which, combined with their particular physique, made them exceptional [[Technician/Legends|mechanics]] and [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]]. Kloperians were able to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]]. Their laughter produced a squishy sound, sickening to some non-Kloperian [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. Kloperians expressed indignation by wrapping their tentacles around their body. Some Kloperians had multiple wives.<ref name="TNR" />

==History==
[[File:Kloper_space.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kloper and surrounding space]]
The Kloperians were native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kloper]],<ref name="TNR" /><ref name="AGttSWU">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> within the [[Kloper system/Legends|eponymous]] [[Star system/Legends|star system]], a part of the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> right on the border with the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]]-ward [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies Region]].<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> They were active in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]],<ref name="TNR" /> at which [[Time/Legends|time]] many worked for the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]],<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> earning a reputation as some of the best mechanics and engineers in the Republic.<ref name="TNR" />

===Sabotaging the Republic===
{{Quote|You want to explain to me why you're illegally jacked into our computer system?|A Kloperian mechanic confronts R2-D2|<ref name="TNR" />}}
That year, the Republic implemented an upgrade of its entire [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|corps]], including eliminating the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] sockets in its [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]]. A contingent of over 50 Kloperians worked on these upgrades, and they were the only species authorized to work on the new fighters. Unknown to Republic leadership, the upgrades included [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Bomb/Legends|detonators]] as part of a plan by the unstable [[darksider]] [[Kueller]] to cripple the Republic. In order to ensure the secrecy of their [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]], the Kloperians strictly prohibited astromech droids from their maintenance bays.<ref name="TNR" />

When [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] authorized routine maintenance on his X-wing, the Kloperians set about entirely disassembling the starfighter, as, on the orders of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], any routine maintenance was to include upgrading X-wings to clean fighter status. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] astromech unit, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] went to investigate the modification. While R2-D2 was jacked into a [[computer terminal]] he was discovered by a [[Unidentified Kloperian mechanic|Kloperian mechanic]], who pulled the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and his companion, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], into the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] bay and locked them inside a closet. When the Kloperians finally checked their serial numbers, they released the droids, showing them Skywalker's dismantled starfighter. Suspicious of the Kloperians' true motives, R2-D2 took the precaution of removing the X-wing's [[Memory chip/Legends|memory chips]]—R2-D2 and the X-wing had been paired together for so long that they had developed their own special language.<ref name="TNR" />

[[File:R2_D2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|R2-D2 foiled the Kloperians' sabotage]]
Hearing of the disassembling of his fighter, Skywalker visited the hangar himself, ordering [[Human/Legends|Human]] mechanic [[Cole Fardreamer]] to reassemble the X-wing. At one point, Fardreamer tried to recruit a Kloperian to help, but R2-D2, who was helping with the reassembly, made a significant disturbance until Fardreamer abandoned the idea. Eventually, the pair discovered a detonator hidden deep within the newly-installed [[Computer/Legends|computer]] system, and later found similar devices in other upgraded and prototype X-wing starfighters.<ref name="TNR" />

However, a [[Unidentified Kloperian guard|Kloperian guard]] discovered Fardreamer inside one of the new X-wings and confronted him at blaster point. When Fardreamer showed the detonating device to the guard, the Kloperian accused the Human of sabotage, and shot at R2-D2 when the droid tried to jack into a computer terminal. The Kloperian was too slow, however; R2-D2 had summoned General Antilles to the hangar. Learning of the detonator, Antilles dismissed the Kloperian guard for shooting the droid. Following this, [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] ordered the Kloperians working in the ship docks relieved of their duties until they could be cleared of tampering.<ref name="TNR" />

===Sabotage foiled===
Following this incident, R2-D2 suspected that the starfighter sabotage of the X-wings was linked with the [[Bombing of the Senate Hall]] that had occurred days earlier. Despite it being restricted to unauthorized personnel, R2-D2 investigated the bomb scene, discovering smaller, but similar, detonators there. The droid was interrupted by a [[Unidentified Kloperian night guard|Kloperian night guard]], who ordered R2-D2 and C-3PO to vacate the premises, but not before R2-D2 had obtained four Imperial detonators. Before they were able to report their discovery, however, Organa Solo resigned her position and left Coruscant to rescue Skywalker, who was being held by Kueller on [[Almania]], leaving [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] in her role as Chief of State. The droids tried to report to Mothma—meeting the Kloperian guard from the starship hangar in her anteroom—however, blocked from meeting her by an [[Mon Mothma's receptionist droid|officious]] [[Secretary droid/Legends|secretary droid]], R2-D2 instead traveled to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Telti/Legends|Telti]], which the droid believed was the origin of the detonators. There, the two droids managed to deactivate all the detonators spread across the galaxy before they could be detonated, thus foiling Kueller's plan and limiting the impact of the Kloperians' sabotage.<ref name="TNR" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Kristine Kathryn Rusch]] created the Kloperians for her novel ''[[The New Rebellion]]'', published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[1996]]. [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] later placed their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]); however, while it appeared on the map of the Inner Rim, the ''Atlas''{{'s}} Appendix placed it in the Colonies Region. The [[June 11]] [[2010]] update to its [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|online companion]] corrected the errant Colonies placement.

Throughout ''The New Rebellion'', Rusch refers to Kloperian individuals using the pronoun, "it." This ambiguity over the gender of individual Kloperians has been maintained in this article, despite one reference by Rusch to "wives."

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Rebellion]]'' {{1st}}
